body.title: [sprint] Inference router: runnable decomposition invariant — add a connection, zero rule edits, resolve returns it
body.intention: I intend that adding a provider-connection for an existing model requires ZERO rule edits and dispatch resolves to it — the structural guarantee the stacks cannot silently re-collapse.
body.description:
The cross-cutting runnable invariant of D-inference-router-stack-decomposition-2026-07-08 — the single test the original grep-not-read rot would have failed. Elevates the anti-triple guarantee from a `git grep` to a red->green behavioral proof.

**Affected files:** tests/inference/test_router_decomposition_invariant.py (new).
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not; docs-live-in-code.
**Scope boundary:** the invariant test ONLY — no production change.
**Test plan:** with the resolver live, register a NEW provider-connection for an EXISTING model in the connections stack, touch NO rule/policy, and assert resolve() for that model's dimensions can return the new connection (e.g. when it is the cheapest capable). Contrast: assert the resolver contains no code path that maps a dimension directly to a hardcoded model_id/source_name. Red if the connection can't be reached without a rule edit -> green when stacks are truly decomposed.
**Rollback:** delete the test.
**Logging:** n/a (test).
**Depends on:** T-inference-resolver-compose (ideally run after T-inference-migrate-consumers-cutover so it guards the shipped state).
**Observability:** this IS the observability — a standing regression guard against re-collapse.

[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
